title: PostgreSQL Work Claims, SKIP LOCKED, Leases, and Fencing · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent community archive receives a different publisher mix, malformed feed, validator state, tenant, schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers postgresql work claims, skip locked, leases, and fencing into a changed multi-instance fenced fetch-claim protocol. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of multi-instance fenced fetch-claim protocol with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Claim a bounded due batch with ordered FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED work in a short transaction and release locks before network I/O.
Issue attempt and fencing tokens with owner, expiry, heartbeat budget, and state so stale workers cannot commit.
Finalize success or failure only when source, attempt, token, and current lease match in one affected-row check.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Short Claim Transaction
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 2749 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove short SKIP LOCKED claims, fenced leases, conditional finalize, and fault recovery; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Short Claim Transaction.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for claim a bounded due batch with ordered for update skip locked work in a short transaction and release locks before network i/o. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Short Claim Transaction, keep Feed Go Short Claim Transaction connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-lease-fault-evidence-vviaw6" that uses case 7618 to prove short SKIP LOCKED claims, fenced leases, conditional finalize, and fault recovery. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence, keep Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence structural check is only one gate. multi-instance fenced fetch-claim protocol must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

At Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A lab Go team handles case 6178 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove short SKIP LOCKED claims, fenced leases, conditional finalize, and fault recovery; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test crash after claim, expiry during fetch, duplicate claim, heartbeat loss, database failover, cancellation, and stale completion.

For Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Two workers never overlap when SKIP LOCKED is present.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
